free_text: Excitation functions and yields for the production
of 87m-Y and preparation of a 87m-Sr generator
0.5 uA beam of 40 MeV 3He particles
from the IPCR cyclotron, or
0.3 uA beam of 60 MeV 4He particles
from the NIRS cyclotron.
The duration of the bombardments was 30-40 min.
About 2 h after EOB the targets were measured.
Rubidium chloride target of 2.0 x 2.0 cm2 and surface
density 2.35 mg/cm2 was melted on iron
foils (4.5mg/cm2). About 10-15 of these targets were
stacked on a brass target-holder with water cooled
pipes.
intrinsic Ge detector coupled to a 4096-channel
analyzer. This system had a 1.98 keV resolution (FWHM)
at 1.33 MeV and peak-to-Compton ratio of 32:1.
Preparation of the generator and anion-
exchange method.
The actual
energy of the particles incident on each target foil
in a stack, which agreed within the experimental
error with the range-energy data described in
CEA-R-3042, was checked with a iron foil activation
technique similar to the method described in:
By CAJAD.
According to MEMO CP-D/161.
According to MEMO CP-C/173.
SD:Updated to new date formats,lower case.
Corrections in all Subents.  Data were re-digitized.
Fig. 1 from Int.J.Appl.Radiat.Isot.,31(1980)399
SD: SF5=IND and SF9=EXP deleted from
REACTION codes. Right hand part of reaction code
was deleted. EN-ERR -> EN-ERR-DIG; DATA-ERR -> ERR-DIG.
No information about source of uncertainty

REACTION codes. Right hand part of reaction code

/By author/.Y-85-m was determined through
both its daughter Sr-85-m,since Sr-85-m comes
through both Y-85-m and Y-85 ,the Sr-85-m which
was formed by decay of Y-85-m was determined by
subtracting the Sr-85-m formed by decay of Y-85
from the gross Sr-85-m.
